name: Flidais
description: Flidais, a prominent figure in Irish mythology, is primarily associated with fertility, cattle, and wild animals. Often described as a powerful and beautiful woman, Flidais's influence extended over matters of prosperity and abundance, making her a significant deity for communities reliant on agriculture and hunting. She is often depicted with a herd of magical cattle, symbolizing her role as a provider and protector. Flidais's connections to both domestic animals and the untamed wilderness highlight a complex understanding of the natural world within Celtic belief systems. Her narratives often involve interactions with mortal men, showcasing her agency and power. The tales surrounding Flidais reveal a deity who embodies both the nurturing aspects of motherhood and the untamed energy of the natural world, highlighting her multifaceted nature within the Celtic pantheon. Some accounts even portray her as a shapeshifter, further emphasizing her connection to the fluid and ever-changing aspects of nature.
subtitle: Goddess of Cattle and the Hunt

keyMyths.title: The Cattle Raid of Cooley
keyMyths.description: In the epic Táin Bó Cúailnge (The Cattle Raid of Cooley), Flidais plays a crucial role. She possesses a magical cow that can provide milk for 300 men at a time. Her affections are sought by various warriors, and she eventually sides with Fergus mac Róich, allowing him and his men to sustain themselves during the raid. This highlights her power and influence, demonstrating her ability to provide sustenance and support in times of conflict.
keyMyths.source: Táin Bó Cúailnge (The Cattle Raid of Cooley)

keyMyths.title: Flidais and Oilill Finn
keyMyths.description: Flidais is said to have had numerous consorts, including Oilill Finn. She demanded a high tribute of cattle from him, showcasing her power and wealth. This tale emphasizes her role as a sovereign goddess, one who commands respect and receives offerings due to her association with abundance and fertility. The story serves as a reminder of the importance of honoring the natural world and its bounty.
keyMyths.source: Various Irish mythological texts and folklore

keyMyths.title: Flidais and the Forest
keyMyths.description: Flidais is deeply connected to the forest, often depicted as roaming through the woods with her animals. She is seen as a protector of the wild creatures and a guardian of the natural world. This association highlights the importance of respecting the environment and the interconnectedness of all living things. Her role as a forest deity underscores the Celtic reverence for nature and its inherent power.
keyMyths.source: Folk tales and interpretations of Irish mythology
